{{Quote|Poor Denin… I feel so sorry for him… for everyone on his planet. What's happening on Naldar sounds a lot like what happened to my homeworld—Belderone—right before I joined the Alliance. The two planets are even close together… I hear a lot of Belderones took refuge on Naldar…|Barney|<ref name="SW92" />}}
'''Naldar''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] in the [[Phelleem sector/Legends|Phelleem sector]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Naldar became the target of severe [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] aggression, withstanding a [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] offensive that [[Death/Legends|killed]] most of the royal family, and a [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] another Imperial attack [[Subjugation of Naldar|subjugated]] the world. However, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Vila]], the royal family's sole survivor, returned with an [[Alliance of Free Planets]] force that ended the Imperial threat on Naldar.

==Description==
A [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Phelleem sector/Legends|Phelleem sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> Naldar was a world comfortably habitable for [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. Naldar was located close to the planet [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]].<ref name="SW92" />

==History==
{{Quote|The Empire… I know the Alliance defeated most of them… but a lot of their forces didn't capitulate. They regrouped on my homeworld&mdashNaldar! They're slaughtering my people…|[[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Vila]]|<ref name="SW92" />}}
Many refugees from Belderone fled to nearby Naldar when that world was [[Battle of Belderone (Galactic Civil War)|subjugated]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="SW92" /><ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|II|357|Naldar}}</ref> after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. Naldar itself was [[Subjugation of Naldar|attacked]] by the Galactic Empire in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]. Although the planet initially managed to resist the invasion, [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Denin]] was killed while defending the rest of the [[King/Legends|royal]] family.<ref name="SW92" />

[[File:Subjugation_of_Naldar.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Alliance of Free Planets forces and Imperial troopers fighting on Naldar.]]
Led by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Flint]], Imperial forces finally managed to conquer the planet after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] Vila, the last survivor of Naldar's royal family, impersonated her [[Death/Legends|deceased]] brother Denin—who had wished to be a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]—and sought aid from the [[Alliance of Free Planets]] on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. She returned with a small force led by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who along with Flint's childhood friend [[Barney]] managed to convince Flint to cease the attack. However, Vila lost her life in the process, as she took a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper's]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolt intended for Flint.<ref name="SW92" />

==Behind the scenes==
Naldar appeared as one of the primary settings of [[Star Wars (1977) 92|''Star Wars'' (1977) 92]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]], illustrated by [[Jan Duursema]] and released in [[1984]].<ref name="SW92" />
